asset_composition.notes: Asset base is dominated by receivables (franchise fees receivable $189K is net of $945K gross offset by $756K commissions payable). Minimal tangible assets — this is an asset-light franchisor.

liability_structure.notes: Liabilities are overwhelmingly deferred franchise fees. This is structurally common for young franchise systems collecting upfront fees recognized over 10-year terms.

leverage_and_solvency.notes: Deeply negative equity. Cash covers approximately half a month of operating expenses. The franchisor depends on ongoing fee collections and/or member contributions to remain solvent.

distributions_concern.notes: Member extracted $150K net from a company running losses and with only $52K in cash. This distribution pattern raises questions about the franchisor's financial sustainability.

revenue_mix_2024.notes: Royalties dominate revenue (63%), indicating the franchisor's income is tied to studio-level performance. Franchise fees (10%) are deferred and recognized over agreement terms.

expense_structure_2024.notes: Management agreement with parent accounts for 69% of operating expenses — $985,736 of $1,419,448. This is the single largest expense and is a related-party transaction.

cash_flow_analysis.notes: Operating cash flow was positive only because of $372K in deferred franchise fees collected. Excluding this non-recurring inflow, operating cash flow would have been approximately negative $251K.

deferred_revenue_schedule.notes: 52% of deferred revenue ($454K) is from franchise units not yet opened. If these units fail to open, the franchisor may need to recognize this revenue differently or potentially refund fees.

annual_minimum_fee_burden.notes: This represents the absolute floor of ongoing fee obligations. Variable royalty above the minimum and insurance add to this. Before rent and labor, a franchisee faces ~$87K/year in fees alone.

breakeven_analysis_estimate.notes: Rough estimate using moderate assumptions for rent ($10K/mo for ~3K sqft at $40/sqft), labor ($20K/mo for ~4 staff), and other expenses. Studios below ~$507K annual revenue may struggle to break even. 7 of 17 reported studios fell below this threshold.

fee_escalation_risk.local_ad_ceiling: $2,300/month (may be reached within a few years)
fee_escalation_risk.software_license: No cap; subject to change on written notice
fee_escalation_risk.notes: If all escalation provisions are exercised at maximum rates, the combined monthly fee burden could increase substantially over the 10-year term.
